content: @Boqun Feng Can one now use those atomics? It confuses me that it's the R4L documentation, but the include in the example is still use std::sync::atomic
No, we cannot use those atomics. Especially for the new code. There may be some old code still using Rust native atomics, but they will be changed soon.
The LKMM atomics are still not merged in mainline, it’ll be there at 6.18-rc1
For documentation, I think rustdoc just generates the whole core crate for the moment, so that’s why you can see those atomics.
Philipp Stanner has marked this topic as resolved.

Boqun Feng said:

For documentation, I think rustdoc just generates the whole core crate for the moment, so that’s why you can see those atomics.

Indeed -- disabling those would be part of the "modularization of core" topic we have had for a long time in the usual lists. I added it explicitly there (https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/issues/514).
@Boqun Feng why no fetch_sub for our rust atomics?
I just flipped my logic :laughing:
